item.title: Ross — The Rest Is Noise
item.description: Author: Alex Ross Title: The Rest Is Noise: Listening to the Twentieth Century Year: 2007 Type: Trade book (Farrar, Straus and Giroux) The book opens in an opera house in Graz on May 16, 1906, and the audience is the argument.

item.title: Horowitz — Dvořák's Prophecy
item.description: Author: Joseph Horowitz Title: Dvořák’s Prophecy: And the Vexed Fate of Black Classical Music Year: 2021 Type: Trade history (W. W.

item.title: Goehr — The Imaginary Museum of Musical Works
item.description: Author: Lydia Goehr Title: The Imaginary Museum of Musical Works: An Essay in the Philosophy of Music Year: 1992 Type: Academic book (Clarendon Press/Oxford; second edition 2007) The book opens with a sentence built to start fights: “Bach did not intend to compose musical works.” Goehr, a Columbia p...

item.title: Horowitz — Classical Music in America
item.description: Author: Joseph Horowitz Title: Classical Music in America: A History of Its Rise and Fall Year: 2005 Type: Trade history (W. W.

item.title: Southern — The Music of Black Americans
item.description: Author: Eileen Southern Title: The Music of Black Americans: A History Year: 1971 Type: Academic history (W. W.

item.title: Taruskin — Oxford History of Western Music
item.description: Author: Richard Taruskin Title: The Oxford History of Western Music Year: 2005 Type: Reference history (Oxford University Press; six volumes) Six volumes, more than four thousand pages, a millennium of music, one author — and the fight starts in the introduction.

item.title: PLACES
item.description: The rooms, corners, and addresses where the MAP’s history happened.

item.title: (1978) Ambient 1: Music for Airports
item.description: Brian EnoReleasedFebruary 1979 (the sleeve’s notes are dated September 1978)LabelPolydor / E.G.
